title: Fix OpenCart migration: 500 on /migration/start + Celery trigger
description:
Fix three bugs in the OpenCart → WifiOBD migration system:

1. POST /migration/start returns 500: PydanticSerializationError because the endpoint has no response_model and returns raw SQLAlchemy List[MigrationJob] objects. Fix: add response_model=List[MigrationJobResponse] to the @router.post decorator.

2. Migration never actually runs: start_migration() in migration_service.py creates jobs with PENDING status but has only a comment '# In a real app, this would trigger Celery tasks here'. Fix: trigger run_migration_task.delay(str(job.id), job.entity.value) for each newly created job (not for active_job that already existed). Import run_migration_task from app.tasks.migration_tasks.

3. update_job_status uses .returning(MigrationJob) with ORM object - RETURNING with ORM object may not work correctly with asyncpg. Fix: after update, do a separate select to reload the job by id.

Files to modify:
- backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py: add response_model=List[MigrationJobResponse] to POST /migration/start
- backend/app/api/v1/admin/migration_service.py: trigger Celery tasks in start_migration()
- backend/app/api/v1/admin/migration_repository.py: fix update_job_status to use select after update instead of RETURNING

Do NOT change migration business logic, OC models, or any other files.
acceptance_criteria: start_migration() calls run_migration_task.delay() for each new job
